reasoning: The Lean signature correctly formalizes all components of the informal statement: `[MetricSpace X]` captures "metric space", `[ConnectedSpace X]` captures "connected", `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` captures "having more than one point", and `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` captures "uncountable". The expression `Countable (univ : Set X)` elaborates to `Countable ↥univ`, which by `countable_univ_iff` is equivalent to `Countable X`, so its negation correctly expresses uncountability. Although `ConnectedSpace` in Mathlib includes `Nonempty X`, this is automatically implied by the "more than one point" hypothesis, so this does not introduce any mismatch with the informal statement.
